quizQuestion:
Dijo que {___}.
(Direct speech: 'Estoy cansado.' He said he was tired.)
explanation: estaba cansado — in reported speech with a past main verb (dijo), present tense shifts to imperfect. 'Estoy cansado' becomes 'que estaba cansado'. This is the most fundamental backshift rule.

quizQuestion:
Me contó que {___}.
(Direct speech: 'Llegué tarde.' He told me he had arrived late.)
sentenceTranslation: He told me he had arrived late.
explanation: había llegado tarde — in reported speech, preterite shifts to pluperfect. 'Llegué tarde' → 'que había llegado tarde'. This shows the past-in-past relationship.

quizQuestion:
Explicó que {___}.
(Direct speech: 'Vendré mañana.' He explained he would come the next day.)
sentenceTranslation: He explained he would come the next day.
explanation: vendría al día siguiente — in reported speech, future (vendré) shifts to conditional (vendría). Mañana becomes al día siguiente in formal backshift. Key B2 reported speech rule.

quizQuestion:
Dijo que {___}.
(Direct speech: 'He terminado el trabajo.' She said she had finished the work.)
sentenceTranslation: She said she had finished the work.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech backshift: present perfect → pluperfect
explanation: había terminado el trabajo — in reported speech, present perfect (ha terminado) shifts to pluperfect (había terminado). 'He terminado' → 'que había terminado'. Both preterite and present perfect shift to pluperfect.

quizQuestion:
Nos contó que {___}.
(Direct speech: 'Vivo en Madrid.' He told us he lived in Madrid.)
sentenceTranslation: He told us he lived in Madrid.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech backshift: present → imperfect (vivo → vivía)
explanation: vivía en Madrid — in reported speech, present (vivo) shifts to imperfect (vivía). 'Vivo en Madrid' → 'que vivía en Madrid'. The backshift shows the time distance from the reporting moment.

quizQuestion:
Prometió que {___}.
(Direct speech: 'Estaré allí.' He promised he would be there.)
sentenceTranslation: He promised he would be there.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech backshift: future → conditional (estaré → estaría)
explanation: estaría allí — in reported speech, future (estaré) shifts to conditional (estaría). 'Estaré allí' → 'que estaría allí'. Prometió que triggers the same backshift rules as other reporting verbs.

quizQuestion:
Dijo que {___} esa tarde.
(Direct speech: 'No puedo venir.' She said she couldn't come that afternoon.)
sentenceTranslation: She said she couldn't come that afternoon.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech backshift: present → imperfect (no puedo → no podía)
explanation: no podía venir — modal verb poder also backshifts in reported speech: no puedo → no podía. Esa tarde replaces esta tarde in backshifted time expressions.

quizQuestion:
Le dijo que {___}.
(Direct speech: '¡Ven inmediatamente!' He told him to come immediately.)
sentenceTranslation: He told him to come immediately.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech: imperative → imperfect subjunctive (ven → viniera)
explanation: viniera inmediatamente — in reported speech, imperatives become imperfect subjunctive. '¡Ven!' → 'que viniera'. The verb is decir que (not pedir que, but the pattern is the same). Venir: imperative ven → imperfect subj viniera.

quizQuestion:
Le pidió que {___}.
(Direct speech: '¡Ayúdame!' She asked him to help her.)
sentenceTranslation: She asked him to help her.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech: pedir + imperfect subjunctive (ayuda → ayudara)
explanation: le ayudara — pedir que + imperfect subjunctive. Pedir que is the reporting verb for requests. The imperative 'ayúdame' becomes 'que le ayudara' in reported speech (imperfect subjunctive + pronoun change).

quizQuestion:
Le aconsejé que {___}.
(Direct speech: '¡Descansa!' I advised him to rest.)
grammarPointLabel: reported speech: aconsejar + imperfect subjunctive (descansa → descansara)
explanation: descansara — aconsejar que + imperfect subjunctive. Aconsejar que (to advise that) uses the same pattern as pedir que. The imperative 'descansa' becomes 'que descansara' in reported speech.

quizQuestion:
Le ordenó que {___}.
(Direct speech: '¡Sal de la sala!' He ordered her to leave the room.)
sentenceTranslation: He ordered her to leave the room.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech: ordenar + imperfect subjunctive (sal → saliera)
explanation: saliera de la sala — ordenar que + imperfect subjunctive. The imperative 'sal' (tú) of salir becomes 'que saliera' in reported speech. Salir → imperfect subj: saliera.

quizQuestion:
Me pidió que {___}.
(Direct speech: '¡No digas nada!' He asked me not to say anything.)
sentenceTranslation: He asked me not to say anything.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech: pedir + negative imperfect subjunctive (no digas → no dijera)
explanation: no dijera nada — negative reported imperative. '¡No digas nada!' (negative tú imperative) becomes 'que no dijera nada' in reported speech. Decir: imperfect subj dijera.

quizQuestion:
Le pidió que {___}.
(Direct speech: '¡Habla más despacio!' She asked him to speak more slowly.)
sentenceTranslation: She asked him to speak more slowly.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech: pedir + imperfect subjunctive (habla → hablara)
explanation: hablara más despacio — pedir que + imperfect subjunctive. The affirmative tú imperative 'habla' → reported speech 'que hablara'. Hablar: imperfect subj hablara.
